The EC 346 A12ST3BP1 wellbore was spud on 10-20-08 at depth of 8673' MD.  The wellbore was TD'd on 10-26-08 at a depth of 9546' MD / 9315' TVD, w/a MW of 13.5 ppg.  The well has successfully been completed as a single, gravel packed, oil completion in the "Lentic 1-3" Sands, with completion operations completed on 11-17-08.
At this time drilling has been suspended.

Well D002 was successfully converted from a dual well to a single well to remediate casing psi issue (APM approved 3/28/08; subsequent RPMs approved 4/21/08; 8/7/08; 8/29/08).  A dry hole tree has been installed under RPM approved 9/24/08 while waiting on the new production tree.  All work has been completed under the above referenced APM/RPMs.  The well is SI and will require a new APM to cleanout and produce.  The attached WBS reflects status of well as of 9/25/08.

ERT spud the SS 224 E-20 well on 07-31-08 and drilled to TD, 11670' MD / 10515' TVD on 08-19-08.  After the well was TD'd, it began to flow. The well was shut-in and drill pipe was stuck with the bit @ 11655' MD.  Part of the BHA left in the hole contained LWD tools that included a radioactive source, which was left at 11540' MD.  The wellbore was plugged back for by-pass operations by setting a 327 cu ft cmt plug in the drill pipe and annulus from 11670' - 10905' MD. Then plugs were set in the drill pipe at 10018' MD and 9985' w/10' of cmt on top of the plug at 9985'.  The DP was backed off at 7001' & a kick off plug set 7001' - 6045'.    Operations concluded on 08-26-08, when the wellbore was by-passed at a depth of 6275'.

Stone Energy has successfully performed zone change operations on the VR 261 OCS-G 03328, Well No. A003ST01 to produce the "C-2 Sand".  Operations began on Dec 14, 2007 and were completed on December 21, 2007.  The previous producing interval in the well, the 8600' Sd. was isolated by setting a magna range bridge plug @ 10808' in the tubing and dumping 20' cmt on top, ETOC @ 10788'.  The tubing was punched @ 10728'-33' exposing the existing "C-2 sd" perforations 10728' - 10746' to production.
